comments.overrides: L7 (2026-06-09 audit): exceljs@4.4.0 pulls a vulnerable nested uuid@8.3.2 (GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq, buffer-bounds check). exceljs imports uuid via the named `require('uuid').v4` export, which uuid@14 still provides, so forcing its nested uuid up to the already-present top-level v14 dedupes to a single patched copy without the breaking exceljs 4->3 downgrade that `npm audit fix --force` proposed. W1-L10 (2026-07-08 Run 2): jest's coverage chain (jest -> babel-jest -> @jest/transform -> babel-plugin-istanbul -> @istanbuljs/load-nyc-config) pulls a nested js-yaml@3.14.2, within the vulnerable <=4.1.1 range of GHSA-h67p-54hq-rp68 (quadratic-complexity DoS via repeated merge-key aliases). Reachability is effectively nil (it only ever parses local .nycrc/package.json config, never attacker input), but the top-level js-yaml dependency is already patched at ^4.2.0 and `.load()` has a compatible signature across both major versions, so forcing the nested copy up to the same version is a free, zero-risk dedupe rather than leaving a scanner-visible stale copy around. `npm audit fix --force` would instead have downgraded jest itself to 25.0.0 (upstream @istanbuljs/load-nyc-config still declares ^3.13.1) -- this override avoids that.
